title: 9 Creating Incident Filters, Data Lists, and Teams
body_text:
9	Creating Incident Filters, Data Lists, and Teams
9.1	Creating Incident Filters
Incident filters are used to display incidents that match pre-defined conditions. An active incident filter applies to all the Incident lists. If a filter is restricted to a team, only those team members will have access to that filter.
To create an incident filter:
Go to Incident Filters on the Settings page:
Select
Create Incident Filter
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Selecting to Create Incident Filter
To configure an incident filter:
Note:	See the Documentation side bar on the Settings page for more details if youâre selecting options that differ from the default settings.
â
Enter a name for the incident filter. See Figure 2.
â¡
Select a mode: Unrestricted, Identifier, or By Type (default is Unrestricted).
â¢
Remove the check mark if you want to include reviewed alerts.
â£
Indicate if you want to filter incidents based on specific times (default is All incidents).
â¤
Select any rules to filter on (optional).
â¥
Select any tags to filter on (optional).
â¦
Select any existing incident filters to exclude (optional).
â§
Select a team if you want to restrict this filter (optional). Only members from the selected team will have access to the filter.
â¨
Select
Create
.
Figure 2: Configuring an Incident Filter
The filter is added to your list of incident filters. To manage existing filters:
â
Select
Edit
to update an incident filter. See Figure 3.
â¡
Select
Delete
to remove an incident filter.
Figure 3: Editing or Deleting an Incident Filter
9.2	Creating Internal Data Lists
Universal watch lists are displayed under Data Lists on the Settings page. These watch lists are built into the system, access is read-only, and they are visible to all financial institutions.
Data lists are used to collect specific values from incidents (e.g., suspicious emails). Users with the Senior Investigator_Tenant role can create data lists for internal use that are editable and only visible to users in your financial institution.
To create an internal data list:
Go to Data Lists on the Settings page. See Figure 4.
Select Create Data List.
Figure 4: Selecting to Create Data List
To configure a data list:
â
Enter a name (must start with a letter and canât contain any spaces). See Figure 5.
â¡
Enter the name of a path, or
â¢
Select the path from the drop-down menu.
Figure 5: Naming Data List and Selecting Path
You can select multiple paths. After each selection:
â
Select
Add Property
. See Figure 6.
â¡
Selected paths are placed under the Add Property field.
â¢
Select
x
to remove a path.
â£
Select
Create
to create the data list.
Figure 6: Adding Property and Creating Data List
The new data list is added and only visible to users from your financial institution. See Figure 7.
â
Select the name of any data list to view the items in that list.
â¡
Select
Edit
to update an internal data list.
â¢
Select
Delete
to remove an internal data list.
Figure 7: Editing or Deleting Internal Data Lists
9.3	Creating Teams
You can create teams to assign incidents to specific users for analysis. You can also add teams to incident filters to restrict which users can access the filters.
To create a team:
Go to Teams on the Settings page.
Select
Create Team
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select Create Team
â
Enter a name for the team. See Figure 9.
â¡
Search for any users not listed under Name.
â¢
Select the box beside the name of users you want to add to this team.
â£
Select
Create
.
Figure 9: Select Users and Create Team
END
